div1.entryFree: Owl, a bird of the genus Strix: Ven. 531. Tp. V, 90. LLL V, 2, 896. LLL V, 2, 896 LLL V, 2, 896 Mids. II, 2, 6. H6C V, 4, 56. Tit. II, 3, 97. Mcb. II, 2, 16. II, 4, 13. IV, 2, 11. Lr. II, 4, 213. Cymb. III, 6, 94. Its cries ominous and portending death: Lucr. 165. H6A IV, 2, 15. H6C V, 6, 44. R3 IV, 4, 509. Mcb. II, 2, 3. Accompanying evil spirits: we walk with goblins, --s and sprites, Err. II, 2, 192. Term of contempt: good night, my good o. LLL IV, 1, 141. I bade the vile o. go learn me the tenour of the proclamation, Troil. II, 1, 99. to be a dog, a mule, a cat, a fitchew, a toad, a lizard, an o., a puttock, Troil. V, 1, 68. Allusion to a legendary tale: they say the o. was a baker's daughter, Hml. IV, 5, 41 (a baker's daughter, who grudged bread to our Saviour, was transformed by him into an owl).
